description: This is from http://senseis.xmp.net/?MiaiInTheEndPosition but I couldn't find it anywhere else on this site. It's a bit baffling - I really do hope I've not left anything out. Comments welcome as always.
sgf:

How many of white's strings can black kill?FORCE]LB[fh:Black is dead]MA[bc]MA[bd]MA[be]MA[bf]MA[bg]MA[bh]MA[ah]MA[cb]MA[db]MA[eb]MA[fb]MA[gb]MA[hb]MA[ha]LB[fe:Both]LB[ff:Only one]LB[fg:Neither - it's a seki]AP[goproblems]
(;B[fe];W[ge]C[Please show. In a symmetrical position, please play on the left side.]
(;B[ad];W[ac]
(;B[ae];W[da]C[Black dies])
(;B[ca];W[da]C[Black dies])

(;B[ae];W[fa];B[ag]C[White has one live group.However, there is no rush to play this - if White plays first, Black can kill - see the 'seki' branch. So, best to leave this until the end...]))
(;B[ca];W[da]C[Black dies]))
(;W[da]
(;B[ad];W[fa]C[White has one living group. However, there is no rush to play this - if White plays first, Black can kill - see the 'seki' branch. So, best to leave this until the end...])
(;B[fa];W[ad]
(;B[af];W[ca]C[Black dies])
(;B[ea];W[af]C[White lives with points and in seki. Black can do better.]))))

(;B[da];W[ca]C[Black dies])
(;B[af];W[da]
(;B[fa];W[ca]C[Black dies])
(;B[ae];W[fa]C[Black can tenuki now, leaving white with one living group. Well done.

However, there is no rush to play this - if White plays first, Black can kill - see the 'seki' branch or problem 16446. So, best to leave this until the end...RIGHT])
(;B[ag];W[fa]C[Black can tenuki now, leaving white with one living group. Well done.

However, there is no rush to play this - if White plays first, Black can kill - see the 'seki' branch or problem 16446. So, best to leave this until the end...RIGHT]))

(;W[da]

(;B[fa];W[ad]
(;B[af];W[ca]C[Black dies])
(;B[ea];W[af]C[White lives with points and in seki. Black can do better.]))))
